title: malva suffruticosa
intro: Malva suffruticosa, also known as marsh mallow or common mallow, is a perennial plant in the family Malvaceae, commonly found in wetlands and coastal areas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, due to its high mucilage content. The health benefits of Malva suffruticosa include relief from digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, and skin conditions like eczema and acne, as well as soothing coughs and sore throats. Malva suffruticosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, syrup, or ointment to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
